channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Iran open to Spain requests on Hormuz transit
item.description: The Iranian embassy in Spain said on Thursday that Iran would be receptive to any request from Madrid related to the Strait of Hormuz because Spain respects international law, in what is the first such concession offered to an EU state. Spain has a relatively small merchant fleet but was among the

item.title: Iran sees US peace plan as 'one-sided' as Trump presses for deal
item.description: A US proposal for ending nearly four weeks of fighting is "one-sided and unfair", a senior Iranian official told Reuters on Thursday, while US President Donald Trump said Iran must make a deal or face a continued onslaught. The Iranian official said the proposal, conveyed to Tehran by Pakistan, "w

item.title: WTO chief calls for trade overhaul to meet new world order
item.description: The WTO chief called on countries on Thursday to overhaul global trade rules, telling them the old world order had gone for good, following a year of turmoil sparked by US tariffs and wider geopolitical tensions. Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala set out a list of problems facing the World Trade Organization -

item.title: Trump urges Iran to act quickly on ceasefire plan
item.description: US President Donald Trump warned Iran on Thursday to "get serious" about a deal to end nearly four weeks of fighting, after its foreign minister said Tehran was reviewing the US proposal but that there were no talks on winding down the war. Trump's comments came as the economic and humanitarian to

item.title: US to continue Iran strikes, pause applies only to energy sites, Semafor reports
Death toll nearly doubles to 66 in Colombian military plane crash
Trump hints at wind-down of war as US sends more troops and Iran threatens tourism sites
13 US service members killed in conflict with Iran: report
Four US service members killed in plane crash over Iraq
Iran to allow India-flagged tankers through Hormuz: Indian source
FBI warns Iran may attack California with drones
US charter flight repatriating Americans from Middle East
Bill Clinton to lawmakers investigating Epstein: 'I saw nothing'
Takeaways from Trump's State of the Union address to Congress
US military preparing for potentially weeks-long Iran operations
Trump: Too busy to be a defendant but plenty of time to sue
Fire at Korean car parts factory kills 10, injures 59, 4 missing
item.description: A fire broke out around lunchtime at a car parts factory in the South Korean city of Daejeon on Friday, leaving 10 people dead, 25 seriously injured and 34 with minor injuries, the country's safety ministry said on Saturday. Four other workers were still missing, and the government has activated a

item.title: Qatar and Turkey criticise Iran's latest attacks
item.description: Qatari Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani and Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan held a joint press conference earlier on Thursday. Al-Thani, who is also Qatar's foreign minister, said that Iran had destroyed Qatar's trust with its "aggression," and that trust needed to be r

item.title: Independent Hong Kong panel starts hearing into devastating inferno
item.description: Human factors rendered ineffective almost all the fire safety measures at the site of a ​Hong Kong blaze that killed 168 people in November, the lead lawyer for a panel led by a judge that ‌is investigating the inferno said on Thursday. The Independent Committee set up by Hong Kong leader Joh

item.title: Iran war: Most civilians killed in UAE strikes were from Bangladesh, Pakistan and Nepal
item.description: Almost all the civilians killed in Iranian missile and drone attacks in the United Arab Emirates were from Pakistan, Bangladesh and Nepal, with only one victim from outside South Asia. Authorities in the UAE have released the names of the six civilians killed in the attacks. The list shows that on

item.title: At least 10 patients killed in hospital fire in India
item.description: At least 10 people were killed in a fire that broke out in the trauma care unit of a hospital in the eastern Indian state of Odisha on Monday, officials said. Five people were critically wounded, but it was not clear if it was from the fire or earlier injuries. The fire was likely to have been

item.title: Israeli foreign minister says no plans for talks with Lebanese government
item.description: Israeli Foreign Minister ​Gideon Saar said on ‌Sunday that the government was not planning to ​hold direct talks ​with Lebanon in the coming ⁠days and had ​not told the United ​States it was running low on missile interceptors. Israel's Haaretz newspaper ​reported on Saturday ​that Israel and

item.title: North Korea fires possible missile towards sea during US-South Korea drills
item.description: North Korea fired a projectile towards the sea on Saturday, South Korea and Japan said, with Tokyo saying it may have been a ballistic missile, while the US and South Korea conducted military drills. The projectile was fired toward the sea off North Korea's east coast, the South Korean military sa

item.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan urged to resolve tensions via talks
item.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi told his Afghan counterpart Amir Khan Muttaqi in a phone call that disputes between Afghanistan and Pakistan should be resolved through dialogue and consultation, not force, the foreign ministry said in a statement published on Friday. Wang urged both sides to rema

item.title: Iran’s new Supreme Leader safe despite injury reports
item.description: Iran’s new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is reportedly “safe and sound” following unconfirmed reports of an injury during the ongoing war, a government adviser said. Yousef Pezeshkian, son of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, told Instagram followers that he had inquired wit

item.title: Iranian drones strike near Dubai airport, wounding four
item.description: Two Iranian drones struck near Dubai International Airport on Wednesday, injuring four people, authorities said, though flights continued at the world’s busiest international hub. The Dubai Media Office reported minor injuries to two Ghanaian and one Bangladeshi national, and moderate injuri

item.title: Roadside bomb kills two policemen in NW Pakistan
item.description: Two policemen were killed and 32 others, including five policemen and 27 civilians, were injured in an explosion in Pakistan's northwestern Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province on Saturday, police said. The incident happened in a market area in the tribal district of South Waziristan at about 5:00 pm local

item.title: Inside the Nepal campaign that could vault Balendra Shah to power
item.description: The campaign that swept aside Nepal's established political heavyweights and brought 35-year-old rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah to the cusp of power emerged out of the top floors of a six-story building in west Kathmandu. Shah is the candidate for prime minister from the Rastriya Swatantra
